item.title: Hong Kong rejects appeals from seven convicted of 2019 Parliament invasion
item.description:
The Hong Kong court yesterday rejected the appeals presented by seven protesters convicted of riots that occurred during the invasion of Parliament in 2019, at the height of the pro-democracy protests that shook the Chinese region.
Among the protesters who filed an appeal was actor Gregory Wong Chung-yiu, who was contesting his conviction for public disorder and a sentence of six years and two months in prison. The Hong Kong Court of Appeal upheld the decision, unanimously concluding that the involvement in the invasion of the Legislative Council was deliberate and not that of an “innocent bystander”. “Knowing that the complex was occupied, he dressed in black and entered the chamber, where he not only handed over material to journalists, but also maintained physical contact with the protesters, hugging them,” the sentence states. The judges also rejected the claim that the sentence imposed on Gregory Wong was disproportionate. The court also rejected the appeals of six other defendants. Five of them – including former student leader Althea Suen Hiu-nam, who pleaded guilty – only appealed their sentences, which included sentences of up to six years and ten months in prison. The sixth defendant, Amy Pat Wai-fun, contested both the conviction and the sentence of four years and nine months imposed. In February 2024, a lower court judge sentenced 11 defendants to prison terms ranging from four and a half years to six years and ten months. Two journalists, tried together, were fined up to 1,500 Hong Kong dollars for entering or remaining in the complex, although they were not charged with rioting. In its 2024 ruling, the court emphasized the symbolic nature of the attack, stating that participants “torn up copies of the Basic Law and displayed colonial flags,” gestures it deemed “provocative and defiant of authority.” The court also highlighted that the episode “undermined governance capacity and left deep scars on Hong Kong society”. The events occurred on the night of July 1, 2019, on the 22nd anniversary of the transfer of sovereignty from the United Kingdom to China. Hundreds of protesters, wearing helmets and masks, stormed the complex, caused damage and displayed the British colonial flag before being dispersed with tear gas. The invasion was one of the most tense moments of the protests against the extradition law to mainland China. The demonstrations, with millions of participants and demands for universal suffrage, culminated in the imposition, by the Chinese central government, of the National Security Law in 2020. Authorities say the law has restored order, while human rights organizations and Western governments see it as a tool to limit political dissent. Lusa

item.title: The tree as a metaphor for life inspires shared poetry meeting at FRC
item.description:
The Rui Cunha Foundation is hosting today a poetry session entitled “A Poetry Encounter: The Tree and everything they want…”, in anticipation of World Poetry Day and the International Day of Forests, both celebrated on March 21. The event, led by doctor and writer Shee Va, invites the public to share poems of special meaning, with free entry and performed in Portuguese.
Last week saw world literature spread across the city during the Macau Literary Festival. Today, poetry is once again at the center of the cultural agenda. The Rui Cunha Foundation gallery presents a special session that combines two United Nations events: World Poetry Day and International Day of Forests. Starting at 6:30 pm, “A Poetry Meeting: The Tree and everything you want…” is co-organized by the Reading Club of the Associação dos Amigos do Livro and aims to be a sharing space open to the community, where each participant is invited to bring and read poems that have a special meaning in their life, preferably linked to the theme of the “Tree”, although the organization guarantees that this orientation is not restrictive. The session will be led by Shee Va, a gastroenterologist by profession, but also a writer and in-depth knowledge of Chinese culture, who has dedicated himself to spreading China's literary and traditional heritage among Portuguese-speaking communities for several years. Born in Mozambique in 1955, Shee Va graduated in Medicine from the Faculty of Medicine of Lisbon, having arrived in Macau for the first time in 1983. It was in the city, however, that he found, in 2012, the right environment to dedicate himself to writing, publishing works such as “Uma Ponte para a China”, “Espíritos” and “Boi Cabeça, Cavalo Cara”. His work as an author has been recognized across borders, having represented Macau at the IV Virtual Meeting of Lusophone Writers, held in Venezuela in 2023, and recently participating, at the beginning of March, in a session commemorating the centenary of the death of the Portuguese poet Camilo Pessanha, a special date that outlined the pertinent sharing of the art of letters in recent weeks. Today's initiative is part of a broader program that the Rui Cunha Foundation has been developing around books and words. Last Tuesday, March 17th, the same space hosted a parallel session, in Chinese language, also co-organized by the Associação dos Amigos do Livro and the Associação dos Autores de Macau. Now, the focus returns to Portuguese, in a gathering that aims to honor not only the poets, but also the linguistic and cultural diversity that poetry conveys. As the organization of the event highlights, poetry, “with its ability to move, provoke reflections and transmit feelings, has been able to cross centuries and civilizations, being one of the pillars of literary art”. Whether in Camões' sonnets, Liu Yong's classic poems or contemporary free verse, the poetic word remains one of the pillars of artistic expression and the collective memory of people. The format chosen for this session, which favors the active participation of the public, seeks precisely to give voice to this diversity, allowing each participant to contribute a poem that “touches their soul”, be it classic or modern, well-known or anonymous. Shee Va, who will moderate the conversation, has an unusual path that crosses medicine with literature, and which allows her to look at Chinese culture with the depth of someone who has studied it for decades, but also with the critical distance of someone who grew up in Africa and graduated in Europe, as she described in an interview with PONTO FINAL in 2017. This multiplicity of perspectives has marked her literary work, centered on disseminating the Chinese world to the Portuguese-speaking public, but also her action as a cultural promoter in Macau, where he regularly collaborates with institutions such as the Instituto Português do Oriente and the Rui Cunha Foundation itself, serving as the perfect moderator for open sharing between poets, readers or writing enthusiasts, whether local or international. Today's session therefore serves as a solemn moment of celebration and an invitation to the shared discovery of poetry, in an informal and open environment where you can recite a poem out loud. Entry is free and open to all interested parties, whether or not they are familiar with poetry, just show up at the Rui Cunha Foundation gallery and, if they wish, ask for the floor to share a verse that resonated with them. The session will be held in Portuguese.

item.title: Japan postpones resumption of commercial operations at the world's largest nuclear plant
item.description:
Tokyo Electric Power Company yesterday postponed the restart of commercial operations at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa, which has been closed since 2011 following the accident in Fukushima. The restart had already been postponed on February 26th, due to problems with the control bar alarms.
Japanese power company Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) yesterday postponed the restart of commercial operations at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa, the world's largest nuclear plant by capacity, closed since 2011 after the accident in Fukushima. In a statement, TEPCO said that last Thursday, an alarm indicated a small electrical leak in reactor number 6, which began transmitting power on February 9, after 14 years of suspension. After an investigation, the company determined that there was no real leak, but rather a broken conductor that triggered the alarm by mistake, something that “does not directly affect the safety of the reactor”, which remains stable. TEPCO continues to investigate the details of the incident and has not yet announced a new date for the resumption of commercial operations at the reactor. The restart had already been postponed on February 26th, due to problems with the control bar alarms. TEPCO restarted the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant on January 21, 15 years after it was closed in 2011 following the Fukushima nuclear accident. This was the first time that TEPCO, which managed the Fukushima plant before the disaster, reactivated one of its plants since the nuclear accident, caused by the strong earthquake and subsequent tsunami, which hit northeastern Japan on March 11, 2011. The accident led to a nuclear blackout in the country, where the majority of nuclear reactors remain deactivated. Currently, 14 reactors are operational, 19 have suspended their operations and 27 are awaiting dismantling, according to data from the International Atomic Energy Agency. Reactors 6 and 7 at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa have already undergone revisions for their respective reactivation in 2017, but the plant was subsequently ordered to remain shut down due to security gaps against terrorist attacks. In December 2023, the measures adopted were approved and, since then, TEPCO has been going through the necessary procedures to put both reactors into operation. With a capacity of more than eight thousand megawatts, the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant is a fundamental piece in TEPCO's energy supply plan and is in line with the strategy promoted by the Japanese Government of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to boost nuclear plants and meet emissions reduction targets. Lusa

item.title: Sands wants to invest more in Macau
item.description: Las Vegas Sands is considering moving forward with new investments to improve its offer in Macau, said those responsible for the group that owns Sands China. At a J.P. Morgan forum, Patrick Dumont, CEO and president of Las Vegas Sands, and Daniel Briggs, senior vice president for investor relations, indicated that the first target for increased investment in Macau could be the Venetian, followed by the Parisian. In a note cited by the Inside Asian Gaming portal, J.P. Morgan says that the two people responsible for Las Vegas Sands promised to continue focusing their promotional investments and products to attract and retain more high-end bettors, in order to better align with the Macau market. Although the recent improvements at the Londoner and the Four Seasons have represented “positive points” in this promotional strategy, in the medium term, the company “may need to invest more capital to improve its product offering in Macau”, according to J.P. Morgan analysts. Further investment may also be in the plans for Marina Bay Sands in Singapore, with LVS eyeing improvements to the Sky Park, food and beverage options and lobby. It is recalled that Sands China recorded 7.2 billion patacas in profits in the general calculation of 2025, 14.2% less compared to 2024. Net revenue, however, rose 5.1% to 59.8 billion patacas.

item.title: Four Portuguese co-productions highlighted at the Hong Kong Film Festival
item.description:
Four Portuguese co-productions are part of the Hong Kong International Film Festival, which will take place between the 1st and 12th of April. The festival will also present several classic films restored in 4K, including “Aniki-Bóbó”, the first film by Portuguese director Manoel de Oliveira.
Four Portuguese co-productions are part of the Hong Kong International Film Festival (HKIFF), which will take place between the 1st and 12th of April, the organization announced. According to the HKIFF schedule, released on Tuesday, the World Cinema category includes the film “Laughter and the Knife”, the second fiction feature film by Portuguese director Pedro Pinho. The co-production between Portugal, Brazil, France and Romania was a candidate for the Goya 2026 – Spanish film awards – for best European film and for the 2026 European film awards from the European Film Academy. “The Laughter and the Knife”, by Pedro Pinho, with Sérgio Coragem, Cléo Diára and Jonathan Guilherme in the cast, premiered in May at the Cannes Film Festival, in France, earning the actress a representation award in the “Un Certain Regard” section. In the Masters and Authors section, “Magalhães”, by Filipino director Lav Diaz, will be shown, about the Portuguese navigator Fernão de Magalhães, who was the Philippines' candidate for an Oscar nomination for Best International Film. The film, starring Mexican actor Gael García Bernal as the Portuguese navigator Ferdinand Magellan (1480-1521), who embarked on a circumnavigation journey and died in the Philippines. “Magalhães”, with Portuguese co-production by Rosa Filmes, also features actress Ângela Machado, in the role of Beatriz Barbosa, the navigator's wife, and actors Tomás Alves, Rafael Morais, Ivo Arroja and Valdemar Santos. In the same section is the most recent documentary by German Werner Herzog, “Ghost Elephants”, recorded in Angola, and which premiered at the 82nd edition of the Venice International Film Festival. Among the documentaries to be shown at HKIFF is “Amílcar”, which is the result of a five-year work around the independent African politician Amílcar Cabral, made by Spanish director Miguel Eek. The co-production between Spain, France, Switzerland, Cape Verde and Portugal, by Lx Films, has photography direction by João Pedro Plácido, and is nominated for the award for best Ibero-American documentary at the Guadalajara Film Festival, which will take place in April. In the Kaleidoscope – Poesia em Movimento section of the HUFF will be “Last night I conquered the city of Thebes”, by Gabriel Azorín, a Spanish production with the Portuguese First Age. The festival will also present several classic films restored in 4K, including “Aniki-Bóbó” (1942), the first film by Portuguese director Manoel de Oliveira (1908-2015). The HKIFF includes a competition for short films, the Firebirds awards, and one of the nominees is “Samba Infinito”, a co-production between Brazil and France, directed by Brazilian Leonardo Martinelli. Lusa

item.title: Portugal opens two judge vacancies for Macau courts
item.description: The Superior Council of the Judiciary (CSM) of Portugal announced on Tuesday the opening of recruitment of two Portuguese judges for positions in Macau's first instance courts in the civil area. According to the published notice, judicial magistrates with a seniority of more than seven years and less than 15 years can apply, with the appointment being valid for two years, possibly renewable. Applications must be submitted by March 31, and according to the document prepared by the Independent Commission Responsible for the Appointment of Judges of the Macao SAR, the selected magistrates will have a salary and benefits equivalent to those of local judges of the same category and seniority. The salary corresponds to a percentage of the monthly salary of the Chief Executive of Macau, currently set at 268,297 patacas. In addition to remuneration, judges will be entitled to holiday and Christmas allowances, housing or rental and equipment allowances, medical assistance for themselves and their household, as well as judicial holidays of 22 working days per year. Portugal and Macau maintain a judicial cooperation agreement that ensures the continuity of Portuguese magistrates (judges and prosecutors) in the territory, supporting the Portuguese-based legal system. This collaboration focuses on recruitment, training and the exchange of judicial experiences. The only judge from Portugal currently working in Macau is Jerónimo Alberto Gonçalves Santos, judge of the Court of Second Instance, after judge Rui Ribeiro brought forward the end of the special commission to the end of October 2025, which ended in May 2026. In 2024, the CSM rejected the stay of Portuguese judge of the Court of First Instance Carlos Carvalho, who had been in Macau for 16 years and had been invited by the Independent Commission for the Appointment of Judges in the territory to continue for another two years.

item.title: More psychological support services available in the second semester
item.description:
The Government will fund non-profit medical institutions to launch “community psychological support services” in the second half of this year, providing psychological counseling to residents in need. The Health Services say that psychotherapy vacancies in Macau have been increased to 10,000 per year, a number that “already satisfies the population's demand for treatment”, they say.
Alvis Lo, director of Health Services, admits adopting more measures related to mental health, including moving forward with a new community psychological support project. Scheduled for the second half of this year, the launch of “community psychological support services” will be funded by Health Services and will take place in non-profit medical institutions. The authorities indicate that work is “advancing at full steam”, so that differentiated psychological counseling services can be provided to citizens with different needs. The plan was presented by Alvis Lo in a session of the “community exchange activity” of the Social Affairs and Culture department, organized last Tuesday. The session was attended by several directors from the Social Affairs and Culture department, including secretary O Lam. According to Hou Kong newspaper, around 80 residents participated in the session. On the occasion, Alvis Lo emphasized that authorities have launched “several improvement measures” on mental health services, including collaboration last year with community associations to increase the number of places in psychotherapy services to 10,000 per year. The official considers that the number, at this moment, “already meets the treatment needs of the population” of Macau. Considering that psychotherapy services in Macau are sufficient, Alvis Lo stressed that some citizens do not need medical treatment as the situation is not serious. “It was found that the psychological needs of some residents do not yet reach a level that requires the intervention of a psychotherapist, simply needing someone to listen to them, provide them with company and help them to vent,” he said. It should be remembered that the most recent data from the Statistics and Census Services show that, in 2024, outpatient consultations in hospitals in the specialty of psychiatry reached 50,597, almost 2,000 more consultations carried out compared to 2023. In Macau, also in 2024, there were a total of 17 specialist psychiatric doctors working in hospitals. Regarding services aimed at young people, the Psychiatry Service of the CHCSJ of the Health Services has external subspecialty consultations in child and adolescent psychiatry, with more than 5,000 consultations having been provided in 2024. Official statistics also point to 91 suicides in 2024, a historic record since 2003, when the number of deaths by suicide was almost half of 2024. The Health Services stopped publishing suicide monitoring data, information that has been released to the public since 2018, and data was only released until the first quarter of 2025. However, the authorities have not yet provided any official clarification on the decision.
PSYCHIATRIC CLINIC PHARMACY IN TAIPA
In the aforementioned exchange session under the supervision of Social Affairs and Culture, the director of Health Services also stated that the pharmacy in the Psychiatric Clinic Building of the Centro Hospitalar Conde de São Januário (CHCSJ), in Taipa, will come into operation in the second half of this year. Alvis Lo indicated that it was not possible to install a pharmacy due to the limited space in the Taipa Psychiatry Building, which forced users to go to CHCSJ to collect their medications. However, optimization planning has now been completed, meaning that the space on the ground floor of the building can be used to install a pharmacy. The organization had previously revealed that the Psychiatry service in Taipa serves 50 people daily who require medication, which justifies the need to create a pharmacy.

item.title: Fund to diversify economy will optimize employment structure in the Macao SAR, promises Sam
item.description: The Government Guidance Fund recently announced by the Executive, which aims to boost the diversification of the Macao SAR economy, will optimize Macau's employment structure and create more opportunities for “high-quality development” for companies and residents, the Chief Executive promised yesterday. The Executive recently announced the Government Guidance Fund, a fund of 11 billion patacas from the income accumulated over the years of the financial reserve, to promote the diversification of the Macao SAR economy. Yesterday, at the plenary meeting of the Science and Technology Council, the Chief Executive promised that the fund “will play a leading role in directing social capital to priority industries and key projects”, promoting, “robustly”, industrial modernization and the development of scientific innovation, “optimizing the employment structure and creating more high-quality development opportunities for companies and residents of Macau”. On the occasion, Sam Hou Fai also highlighted that the Macao SAR Government will promote the construction of the Macau Science and Technology Research and Development Industrial Park and, in addition, “will rigorously promote actions to attract investment and companies, in order to establish a solid foundation for the development of the technological industry”. Sam Hou Fai highlighted that, with these several “important” projects accelerating their progress, “time is tight and the tasks are heavy”. Ensuring the planning and implementation of these initiatives will be “a critical challenge that we must address urgently”. On the other hand, “effectively managing and promoting international research teams and scientific organizations established in Macau, improving the internationalized scientific and technological services system and cultivating scientific and technological professionals with a global orientation represent new dimensions that require greater reflection and additional efforts”. Furthermore, “we must face deeply rooted issues, such as insufficient support for new business models in emerging industries and their respective platforms, the incomplete effect of the chain on attracting, training, valuing and retaining high-level qualified staff, the lack of space for them to apply their talents, and the uncertain impact of international strategic competition on scientific research and innovation”. “Accelerating the correction of weaknesses identified in development and boosting technological innovation to boost industrial growth will require greater consensus and continuous exploration”, he stressed. Sam highlighted that “the ultimate goal of research and development, as well as investments in the scientific and technological field, is not mere research, nor is investment just to create a superficially attractive technological image”. It must be the “transformation of knowledge into productivity and the industrialization of technologies”, he argued. In the speech given on the occasion, Sam Hou Fai pointed out that the world is facing “unprecedented transformations at an accelerated pace”. “The international panorama appears to be complex and intricate, marked by more intense geopolitical rivalries and the resurgence of unilateralism and protectionism”, warned the Chief Executive, adding that “a new wave of technological revolution and industrial transformation is gaining momentum, making scientific and technological innovation the strategic battlefield in the international arena”. Faced with “profound changes in the external environment and multiple risks and challenges”, Sam Hou Fai argued that “actively identifying changes, adapting and seeking alternatives to respond to uncertainties of different types with the firmness of high-quality development, constitute the strategic basis for facing external risks and challenges, ensuring initiative in development and safeguarding national security”. Thus, the Chief drew attention to the national 15th Five-Year Plan and the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Plan, which will mark “the transition from growth driven by recovery to high-quality development”. Through the 3rd Five-Year Plan, it will be guaranteed that, “in the country's journey to become a scientific and technological power, Macau will not be absent or left behind, but will make distinct contributions”. “Fully taking advantage of its unique advantages of having the homeland as support and having connections with the world, Macau will continue to demonstrate its role as ‘a center, a platform, a base'”, reiterated the Government leader, also mentioning that, together with the Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, “it will consolidate and strengthen its development, thus promoting the adequate diversification of Macau's economy and supporting the creation, in Macau, of a hub of top qualified professionals at an international level.” “Opportunities and challenges coexist with heavy responsibilities on our shoulders, we have to move forward with determination”, he asked the remaining members of the Science and Technology Council.

item.title: China will not help the US in Hormuz and welcomes the postponement of Trump's visit
item.description:
Beijing will not help Washington reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and will welcome the postponement of US President Donald Trump's visit to China, when the US risks becoming entangled in the Middle East, analysts say.
The war against Iran, now in its third week, faces increasing pressure: oil has stopped flowing through the strait and United States allies refuse to join efforts to guarantee its security. This raises fears that China, the US's main geopolitical rival, could benefit from a conflict that some consider miscalculated. “Trump’s request to postpone the summit with Xi Jinping shows that he underestimated the consequences of Operation Epic Fury,” said Ali Wyne, an analyst at the International Crisis Group, cited by the Associated Press. “The US cannot reopen the strait alone and now needs its main rival to manage a crisis it created,” he pointed out. The Chinese Foreign Ministry avoided responding directly and limited itself to calling for an immediate end to military operations, the containment of escalation and the protection of the global economy. Beijing, which never confirmed the visit on March 31, was available to reschedule, stressing that the parties “maintain communication” and that the postponement is not linked to the request regarding Hormuz. Trump stated that the Chinese “didn’t mind” the postponement of his first official visit to the country in his second term and said he maintained “a very good working relationship with China”. On Sunday, China sent $200,000 in humanitarian aid to Iran, intended for families of victims of a bombing at a school in Minab. The postponement of the visit is welcomed by both sides, said Brett Fetterly, of The Asia Group, noting that Washington faces political constraints and Beijing gains time to better understand Trump's intentions. Recent bilateral trade talks in Paris have yielded few results, with the two countries maintaining divergences on trade, technology and economic security. The North American business community fears that there is not adequate context and preparation for concrete agreements. The transfer of military assets from the Indo-Pacific to the Middle East still raises fears in Southeast Asia of a strategic diversion by the United States. “The longer the war lasts, the greater the concern among Asian allies about US distraction and resource limitations,” said Zack Cooper of the American Enterprise Institute, cited by the AP. The delay could also delay arms sales to Taiwan. “China benefits while the US gets tangled up in the Middle East,” Cooper said, adding that Beijing can simply “let the US hurt itself.” Lusa

item.title: Macau International Parade with voting for most popular group and photography contest
item.description: In this year's edition of the Macau International Parade, which will take place on March 29th, the public will be able to choose the most popular group and there will also be a photography competition. The Cultural Institute (IC) will promote the “2026 Macau International Parade Photo Contest”, so residents can participate in this cultural event. The competition is open to all Macau residents aged 12 and over, and the works submitted must be themed around the wonderful moments of this year's parade. Each participant can send a maximum of five photographs from March 29th to April 6th. Within the scope of this competition, a 1st place, a 2nd place and a 3rd place will be awarded, with 15 honorable mentions being awarded. The 1st, 2nd and 3rd place prizes cannot be awarded to the same participant, with each participant being able to receive a maximum of two prizes. On the other hand, this year's parade will again launch voting for the most popular group. To vote, you must access the Parade's official website from 10am on March 24th and until 5:30pm on March 29th or scan the QR code on the promotional materials available along the Parade route on the day of the event. Members of the public will be able to vote for their favorite artistic group and be in with a chance of winning a round-trip economy class ticket from Macau to any destination operated by Air Macau. This year's parade will bring together more than ten artistic groups from countries and regions coming from centers of the Maritime Silk Road in Asia, Europe and Africa, as well as almost 50 local groups, for an estimated total of around 1,600 artists, “providing a wonderful spectacle”, says the IC.

item.title: Significant increase in flu cases leaves authorities on alert
item.description: Flu activity is increasing. According to Health Services, the positivity rate was at 5% at the end of February, but is now at 17.8%. This percentage is already at the alert level, which is why the Health Services ask the population to take precautions. The Health Services are asking the population to take precautions against the flu due to the significant increase in the positivity rate. The results of laboratory tests show that the positivity rate for the flu virus increased from 5% at the end of February to 17.8% last week, which is already considered an alert level, according to the Health Services. More than 70% of these cases involved the influenza B virus and the Health Services say that data from emergency departments at Centro Hospitalar Conde de São Januário, Kiang Wu Hospital and the new hospital on the Islands show that the number of emergency room visits has gradually increased since the end of February, rising from 177 visits to 342 visits per week last week. Visits for flu-like illnesses among adults have remained relatively stable. There was also a significant increase in flu cases among children. Last Tuesday alone, four cases of collective infection were recorded in local schools, involving a total of 42 children. However, there were no serious cases resulting from flu infections, the Health Services assured. In a statement released yesterday, Health Services asked residents to take precautions, especially pregnant women, young children, the elderly and people with chronic illnesses who have not yet been vaccinated. “Infection with the flu virus increases the risk of serious complications and even death; therefore, vaccination is recommended as early as possible”, appeal the authorities. Therefore, to avoid contracting the flu or other infectious diseases of the upper respiratory tract, in addition to getting the flu vaccine, the following measures must be taken: get the Covid-19 vaccine “as soon as possible”; get enough sleep, have a balanced diet and practice moderate physical exercise; maintain good personal hygiene habits, with special attention to hand hygiene; cover your mouth and nose with a tissue when sneezing or coughing; If there is no tissue, you must cover your mouth and nose with your arm and not with the palm of your hand; maintain good air circulation and environmental hygiene; avoid places with a lot of people; wear a mask if you have cold symptoms, need to care for someone who is sick or enter hospitals and clinics; and, if you feel unwell, seek medical assistance immediately and rest at home. If symptoms worsen, seek medical attention immediately.

item.title: Government removes total ban on smoking in public spaces
item.description:
The Government admits it is difficult to impose a total ban on smoking in public spaces, as it would lead to illegal tobacco consumption. It also indicates that there are no plans to increase tobacco tax in Macau for fear of smuggling becoming serious. Meanwhile, the Office for the Prevention and Control of Smoking and Alcoholism intends to launch designated smoking points in the NAPE area, with the installation of partitions.
A total and widespread ban on smoking is not, for now, viable in the territory, assumes the Office for the Prevention and Control of Smoking and Alcoholism, which considers that the measure is difficult to implement and could lead to illegal tobacco consumption. "A total and immediate ban would lead to illegal consumption, causing greater risks to society. The Government intends to control smoking gradually, with the ultimate vision of a tobacco-free Macau", said Lam Chong, head of the Office for the Prevention and Control of Smoking and Alcoholism. Lam Chong was yesterday on the morning program “Forum Macau” on the Chinese channel Radio Macau, where he was questioned by several residents about the possibility of a total ban on smoking in public spaces in Macau, as well as an increase in tobacco excise tax. Currently, tobacco excise tax in Macau represents 60% of the retail price, higher than that in neighboring regions, particularly in mainland China. The World Health Organization standard, however, is 75% of the retail price. The last time Macau increased this tax was in July 2015. "Tobacco taxes in neighboring regions are even lower. If the local tax is increased too much, it is believed that it will worsen smuggling, which is why a global assessment is necessary", he pointed out. Regarding the problem of secondhand smoke caused by smoking outdoors while walking, Lam Chong said it is difficult to have a complete resolution, and that, currently, “no country or region is able to implement a single prohibition measure”, he said. The Government chooses, instead, to review legislation to designate no-smoking zones in certain high-traffic areas. “We understand that the measures do not fully meet citizens’ expectations, but we hope to first solve the problem of smokers who smoke while walking in densely populated areas,” he stressed. Lam Chong added that the Government will “simultaneously convey to smokers the important message that such behavior is undesirable”, in the hope that they will “moderate their behaviour”.
DESIGNATED SMOKING POINTS
The Health Services recently established an area of ​​10 meters on each side of the doors of three educational establishments and a daycare center as the no-smoking area, also implementing the “no-smoking zone” in the streets surrounding Parque Dr. Carlos d’Assumpção, carried out on an experimental basis. In the Dr. Carlos d’Assumpção Park area, there are 9 designated smoking points, delimited by a dotted white line where rubbish bins with ashtrays are placed. Lam Chong, in this case, revealed that among the smoking points, one will have, on an experimental basis, the installation of partitions. "The authorities are purchasing the aforementioned partitions. The smoking point in question will have capacity for 3 to 4 people", he added. The height of the partitions will “reach the level of the smokers' heads, in order to reduce the dispersion of smoke into the surrounding environment”, he highlighted. The Government will subsequently analyze whether smoking points with partitions can reduce the impact of smoke in surrounding areas. Lam Chong noted, however, that the installation of partitions at designated smoking points will depend on actual conditions, as most streets in Macau are narrow and the installation of partitions may obstruct traffic visibility or pedestrian circulation.

item.title: Seoul says it is “the priority” for the UAE, which will supply 24 million barrels of oil
item.description: South Korea stated yesterday that the United Arab Emirates (UAE) will give priority to Seoul in oil supplies, after Abu Dhabi guaranteed to supply 24 million barrels of crude, in the context of the blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE promised that “no country will receive crude oil before South Korea”, said the chief of staff of the President of the South Korean Republic, Kang Hoon-sik, at a press conference held in Seoul to report on the results of his visit to the West Asian country. Abu Dhabi has stated that Seoul is the UAE's "number one priority" in oil supplies, he added. The agreement provides for the import of an additional 18 million barrels, in addition to the six million previously announced. The official highlighted that, given the effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, it is necessary to guarantee imports through alternative supply routes to overcome the current energy crisis. South Korea largely depends on the energy supply of this war-torn region, from where it imports around 70% of its crude oil and around 20% of its liquefied natural gas. Faced with the risk of supply interruptions, Seoul adopted several measures, such as the introduction of a temporary limit on fuel prices, the extension of diesel subsidies and the preparation of energy saving measures and a supplementary budget to mitigate the economic impact. South Korea will also gradually release, over the next three months, 22.46 million barrels of crude oil from its strategic reserves, as part of the plan coordinated by the International Energy Agency (IEA) to stabilize the global energy market.
